title: MedAdvisor Encounter Rubric
description: One rubric per encounter type. Encodes a medical educator's mark scheme as behavioral, evidence-bearing criteria. This is the source of truth — the model applies it, it does not invent criteria.

encounterType.description: The clinical scenario this rubric covers, e.g. 'Breaking bad news'

version.description: Semver, bumped on any criterion change. Lets a score be tied to an exact rubric version.

source.description: Attribution + sign-off. Proves the rubric is the director's framework.

signedOff.description: True once the director confirms 'yes, this is my framework'

dimensions.description: Top-level scoring axes, e.g. communication vs clinical conduct.

criteria.description: The behavioral items. Each is a yes/no judgment the model must back with a quote.

prompt.description: The behavioral question, phrased so a clear answer is yes/no. E.g. 'Did the clinician check the patient's prior understanding before sharing the news?'

responseType.description: boolean_with_evidence is the default and most reliable for small models.

scale.description: Only for scaled_with_evidence. Keep anchors behavioral.

anchors.description: Map of score -> what that score behaviorally means

whatGoodLooksLike.description: Concrete description the model is told to look for. Director-authored.

requiredElements.description: For clinical-conduct items: the SPECIFIC things that must be addressed (e.g. red flags), spelled out by the director so the model checks presence rather than recalling medicine from its weights.

exemplarQuotes.description: Optional example transcript phrases that would satisfy this criterion. Improves model matching and is great few-shot material.

criticalFail.description: If true, failing this caps the overall score (safety-critical items, e.g. missed red flag).

method.description: weighted_dimension = per-dimension weighted average; checklist_percentage = % of criteria met.

passThreshold.description: Optional overall pass line agreed with the director.
